business_problem: Pinterest needed to scale AI-powered visual discovery to 600 million monthly active users while keeping the platform a positive, trusted, and safe experience differentiated from competitors who optimize for viral engagement.
solution_summary: Pinterest, serving 600M monthly users, runs its AI-powered visual discovery platform entirely on AWS, processing 500+ petabytes of data and over 10M AI recommendations per second. business_problem: Amagi supports 8,000+ streaming channels, 300+ distributors, and 26B annual ad impressions for 45% of the world's top media companies, but its legacy stack (Dataproc for batch, Snowflake for analytics, in-house platforms) created data silos with separate governance models, conflicting numbers across Finance, Operations, and Product, and complex cross-region governance across AWS and GCP.
solution_summary: Amagi rebuilt its data platform as the Amagi Data Platform (ADP) on Databricks, consolidating Dataproc, Snowflake, and multiple in-house systems into a single Lakehouse spanning AWS and GCP. Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines ingest content metadata, asset catalogs, advertising data, service streams, program schedules, and user data into governed Delta Lake tables, where Unity Catalog enforces lineage, access controls, sensitive-data tagging, and cross-cloud compliance. Finance, Operations, and Product teams query the same source of truth through Databricks SQL warehouses, Genie natural-language sessions, and notebooks running on serverless compute, while customer-facing data products for streaming platforms, ad-tech partners, and content owners are derived from the same governed Lakehouse, eliminating reconciliation between systems.

business_problem: Baylor's Enrollment Management division serves 19,000 students and 50,000 annual applications, with a small contact-center team handling 100–300 calls per day about financial aid and student accounts. Dedicated QA could only cover about 5% of calls, feedback often arrived days after escalations, FERPA constraints prevented sending student data to external models, and the richest voice-of-student insights stayed trapped in audio recordings.
solution_summary: Baylor's Enrollment Management team built an agent workflow on Agent Bricks (Mosaic AI) that pulls call recordings and metadata from the contact-center phone system into a medallion architecture on Databricks. A Knowledge Assistant grounded in Baylor's policies and procedures gives representatives in-call guidance, while a Multi-Agent Supervisor scores every call against standard operating procedures, surfaces sentiment trends and Q&A patterns, and links findings to the original transcript with timestamps and source citations. Unity Catalog provides FERPA-aware governance, with row-level security tied to Active Directory groups so different departments see only the calls relevant to their work. Supervisors query call data on demand in natural language through Databricks Genie and generate daily summaries in about two minutes.
business_outcome: Baylor moved from ~5% QA sampling to 100% call review coverage without adding headcount, generates a daily volume/sentiment/exemplar report in about two minutes, and delivers timely coaching tied to specific moments in calls — all inside a FERPA-safe, Unity Catalog–governed environment.

business_problem: Elliptic's clients—compliance teams at crypto exchanges, banks, payment providers, and institutional custody platforms—face rising alert volumes and stringent regulation, and analysts must document each decision by reconciling exposure values, addresses, and transaction chronology. Applying LLMs to sanctions, terrorist-financing, and child-exploitation workflows required strong assurances against hallucination, omission, and opaque reasoning before outputs could feed formal investigations or regulator-facing reports.
solution_summary: Elliptic extended its existing Data & Intelligence Platform with Agent Bricks to build Elliptic's copilot — an agent that describes wallet activity, highlights exposure paths, and produces neutral, report-ready risk narratives aligned to each client's risk appetite. The copilot is backed by Databricks Foundation Model APIs and Model Serving, and every interaction is captured by MLflow tracing so each prompt, intermediate step, and final narrative can be audited end-to-end. Automated evaluation pipelines score responses for correctness, relevance, and safety using LLMs-as-judges, with custom domain-specific safety scorers that distinguish describing money-laundering typologies (appropriate) from promoting harmful activity (inappropriate). Prompt and safety controls are versioned in MLflow so the team can iterate without rework.
business_outcome: Elliptic delivered a compliance copilot in production without building its own governance and traceability stack, helping clients resolve high-risk screenings up to 2x faster and cut Suspicious Activity Report time by 40%, while keeping outputs grounded, evaluated, and regulator-defensible.

business_problem: HP Indigo's industrial print business was managing 3,500+ data volumes (~10TB) across hundreds of jobs spanning ERPs, manufacturing platforms, and field data sources, with critical data tracked in manual spreadsheets. Teams spent days tracing parts and analyzing data across disconnected systems, sometimes halting production while waiting for answers, and there was no single source of truth.
solution_summary: HP Indigo is unifying more than 3,500 data volumes (~10TB) from ERPs, manufacturing platforms, field data sources, and previously-manual spreadsheets onto Databricks under Unity Catalog governance, replacing fragmented legacy warehouses. Lakehouse Federation covers data that still lives outside Databricks, while Unity Catalog provides end-to-end lineage that tracks consumables from manufacturing through customer-site consumption — collapsing a 2-3 day traceability process to 60 minutes. Databricks AI/BI and AI/BI Genie deliver trusted analytics alongside existing Tableau and Power BI, with Genie surfacing natural-language access on top of Unity Catalog-governed data using role-level security and simplified naming conventions. Delta Sharing distributes governed data internally, with plans to extend to external vendors. A first prediction model running on Databricks lifted manufacturing yield from 60% to 92%.
business_outcome: HP Indigo cut consumable traceability from 2–3 days (often pausing production) to about 60 minutes using Unity Catalog lineage, and used a Databricks-built prediction model to lift manufacturing yield on a key part from 60% to 92% — while AI/BI Genie removed the dependency on technical teams for routine data questions.

business_problem: NAB had standardized on Databricks but its 300–400 data engineers were building 1,600+ Spark pipelines in inconsistent ways, with hand-written Spark and legacy SQL DMLs of 5,000–8,000 lines and some pipelines containing 64 union-alls. The bank needed a single, governed way to run, standardize, and productionize Spark workloads from ingestion to transformation without slowing teams down.
solution_summary: National Australia Bank (NAB) is consolidating 1,600+ hand-written Spark and legacy SQL pipelines onto Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines on Databricks — 1,800 declarative pipelines run today — to give 300–400 engineers a single, consistent way to build, run, and operationalize Spark workloads. Engineers define what the data should look like (target tables, merge keys, business logic); Lakeflow handles orchestration, scaling, incremental processing, and state management, while AutoCDC eliminates the thousands of lines of hand-coded SCD logic that previously spanned Spark and SQL. Bronze ingestion is 100% declarative, ~50% of the Silver layer is migrated, and Gold is the next target — moving the bank toward a fully declarative, streaming-first architecture with under-15-minute end-to-end latency across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
business_outcome: NAB collapsed multi-thousand-line pipelines (some 64 union-alls down to 3), raised pipeline success rates from 86% to 99.6%, improved data quality by 38%, and brought end-to-end latency under 15 minutes — with 100% of Bronze pipelines and roughly 50% of Silver pipelines now running declaratively.

business_problem: Raiffeisen Bank International (RBI) operates a highly federated network of banks across many countries with stringent European banking requirements for security, governance, and auditability. Decades of organic growth left a fragmented analytics environment of on-prem and cloud systems with their own SQL dialects, access controls, and operational models — limiting collaboration, code reuse, and group-level governance, with one query taking 30 days on the legacy infrastructure.
solution_summary: Raiffeisen Bank International consolidated a fragmented analytics environment — on-premises and cloud systems with different SQL dialects, access controls, and operational models across federated banks in Central and Eastern Europe — into the APEX decentralized analytics platform built on Databricks SQL. The rollout was deliberately phased: legacy platforms ran in parallel while users were onboarded gradually and production workloads were protected. Once on Databricks SQL, queries that previously took up to 30 days dropped to ~12 minutes, average workloads run 3–4x faster, and BI is served directly from the Lakehouse without unnecessary data movement. Elastic compute scales on demand for hundreds of concurrent risk, compliance, finance, and retail analysts. Centralized access controls, audit logs, and lineage meet stringent European banking governance requirements, while open formats and APIs avoid future lock-in.
business_outcome: On Databricks SQL, RBI's average workloads now run 3–4x faster than on the previous cloud solution and time to insight is 30–40% shorter, with one extreme legacy query dropping from 30 days to about 12 minutes. Analytics TCO is 5x lower than the previous cloud solution, achieved through architectural simplification rather than usage caps, while audit, lineage, and openness satisfy European banking regulators.

business_problem: SSE Airtricity serves around 750,000 customers across the island of Ireland with smart meters widely deployed, but generic energy tips and black-box third-party tools fell short of personalized insights. The Data & Analytics team needed a fully governed, in-house generative AI solution able to ingest billions of smart-meter readings (48 daily readings per meter) and deliver trusted, auditable recommendations.
solution_summary: SSE Airtricity built the in-house Enhanced Smart Insights (ESI) Energy Advisor on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform — already on Azure after migrating from Oracle, with Unity Catalog providing centralized governance for both data and AI models and Delta Lake as the storage foundation. Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines ingest billions of rows of smart-meter data nightly (48 readings per meter per day across 65,000+ customers) and feed personalization models that call Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 through Databricks AI Model Serving. An evaluation-first MLflow GenAI framework with custom AI judges defined alongside human feedback automatically rejects and regenerates any insights that fail on length, language, tone, or accuracy before they reach customers. Custom Databricks Apps surface real-time monitoring of cost, model performance, and judge failure rates with automated alerts. The team went from concept to production in four months.
business_outcome: Soft-launched in mid-December 2025, the ESI Energy Advisor reached production in four months, drove a 12% increase in unique visitors to the insights section in the first month, and now engages 90% of logged-in customers with personalized recommendations — delivering bill-day insights vs. competitors' 120-day wait and laying the foundation for tariff recommendations, bill analyzer, peer comparisons, and energy disaggregation.

business_problem: SūmerSports helps NFL and college football teams make multimillion-dollar roster decisions by aggregating 14 data sources (player tracking, scouting, client data, NFL contracts) at up to 200TB per season. Its pre-Databricks stack was a fragmented patchwork of Azure-native services, open-source tools, and custom orchestration with no formal lineage or governance, forcing overly broad access patterns and making data engineering the bottleneck.
solution_summary: SūmerSports rebuilt its football-analytics stack on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform, consolidating fragmented Azure-native services, open-source tools, and custom orchestration into a single foundation that handles ~200TB of structured and semi-structured data per NFL season from 14 sources (sensor tracking, scraped web data, in-house scouting evaluations, client data, NFL contract financials). Unity Catalog governs the data sources with curated, tagged datasets and lineage; MLflow tracks experiments and prototypes; Databricks Asset Bundles let data scientists deploy model training, cleaning, and inference jobs as infrastructure-as-code; ~95% of workloads run on serverless compute. AI/BI Dashboards, Genie, and the Databricks Assistant surface analytics to ~60 internal users and to customers. Embeddings for the SūmerBrain chatbot are prototyped on Mosaic AI and served in production through AWS Bedrock. Time-to-market dropped from 3 months to 4 weeks, and post-game insights from 4 days to under a minute.
business_outcome: SūmerSports cut time-to-market for new models from three months to four weeks (3x), accelerated post-game player insights from four days to within minutes, scaled to roughly 3x the weekly NFL data volume, and runs ~95% of workflows on serverless — letting ~50 technical and ~10 non-technical users self-serve insights from a shared governed platform.

business_problem: Deutsche Börse Group's exchange, clearing and post-trade infrastructure — including Clearstream's central securities depository processing over one million transactions per day across more than 60 markets, SAP S/4HANA and the DAX index calculation — ran on stable but rigid on-premises systems where provisioning new environments took weeks or months, hardware bounded release cycles, and the platform was no longer keeping pace with evolving regulation and client demands for faster innovation.
solution_summary: Deutsche Börse Group migrated mission-critical post-trade and DAX index systems to Google Cloud, replacing on-premises infrastructure with a scalable, latency-sensitive architecture. Financial transaction data flows through Compute Engine instances (N/C series machines) running settlement and index calculation workloads, backed by Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme for consistent millisecond-level database performance. BigQuery ingests market indices and transaction analytics. The infrastructure supports 13,000+ daily DAX index calculations, 30M ticks, processing over 1M transactions daily while maintaining regulatory compliance and enabling provisioning times to drop from months to hours.
business_outcome: Deutsche Börse Group lowered total cost of ownership, sharply improved settlement and DAX-index latency, cut SAP disaster-recovery time, and turned infrastructure provisioning from a months-long bottleneck into an on-demand capability that no longer limits delivery of new financial services.

Thousands of CPU cores and 60+ apps migrated
85% reduction in disaster recovery time for the critical SAP S/4HANA environment
50% less latency for the DAX index engine handling 13,000+ indices and ~30M ticks daily
More than 75% of workloads running in the cloud
Provisioning time reduced from months to hours
Development cycles for new financial services shortened from months to days

Employees use Gemini Enterprise agents to automate ad creation, wellness consultations via body composition analysis (Tanita API integration), and inventory search using local context. Video and image generation (Veo 3 and Imagen 4) create promotional content.

business_problem: Hershey Entertainment & Resorts (HE&R) needed to personalize experiences for highly varied guests across an amusement park, hotel, hockey team and entertainment venues, but its prior reliance on manual data sampling from Google Analytics and call recordings meant it could only review small slices of customer signals. The contact center also absorbed high volumes of repetitive calls — particularly seasonal-employee shift call-offs and password resets — diverting agents from complex guest issues.
solution_summary: Hershey Entertainment & Resorts built a comprehensive customer and employee engagement platform using BigQuery, Gemini, Conversational Agents, and the Customer Engagement Suite IVR ('Agnes'). Guest service data from calls and web activity aggregates into BigQuery, where Gemini analyzes sentiment, engagement surveys, and interaction patterns. Conversational Agents power a chatbot deployed on the website within 40 hours to handle invoice requests and password resets. The Customer Engagement Suite deploys 'Agnes', an agentic IVR system that recognizes callers, automates employee call-offs (25%+ of volume), and manages password reset requests (50%+ of calls) via webhook integrations to internal systems.
business_outcome: HE&R automated a large share of repetitive guest and employee contact-center interactions, freed staff to focus on complex requests and innovation, and shortened the loop from raw interaction data to actionable reporting.

solution_summary: Sabre launched SabreMosaic, an AI-powered travel commerce platform underpinned by Sabre IQ, an intelligence layer built on Gemini models and Vertex AI. The platform processes 50+ petabytes of anonymized travel data to deliver personalized offers, ancillary upsells (10% revenue lift), seat upgrade optimization (20% revenue lift), and real-time market forecasting. SabreMosaic Concierge IQ, built using Vertex AI Agent Builder, deploys conversational agents that handle multi-leg trip planning, rebooking, hotel arrival management, and expense filing in one seamless chat interface. Google Maps Platform provides location services, and the Travel Impact Model API enables carbon footprint display for sustainable travel options.
business_outcome: Sabre is monetizing personalization for airlines, accelerating customer-care productivity, and shipping new agentic experiences on a modular API-based platform while running on a far cheaper and more resilient cloud foundation.

business_problem: Shopify powers millions of merchants in more than 175 countries whose traffic can spike within seconds — particularly during Black Friday and Cyber Monday — and traditional static environments would have required costly overprovisioning to survive peak demand without compromising reliability for 81M buyers.
solution_summary: Shopify's commerce platform processes record-breaking transaction volumes on Google Cloud during Black Friday weekend 2025, handling $14.6B in global sales and 489M requests per minute at peak. Compute Engine runs general-purpose commerce services on N2/N4 machines, C4 instances power Shop App catalog search, and Z-series machines provide high-performance local storage for real-time package tracking across 136M deliveries. Google Kubernetes Engine orchestrates auto-scaling microservices to absorb traffic spikes instantly. Hyperdisk Balanced and Extreme store databases supporting 14.8T queries and 1.75T writes during the weekend. AI workloads leverage accelerator-optimized VMs to power Shopify Magic recommendations and Sidekick assistant features.
business_outcome: Shopify absorbed record peak commerce traffic without overprovisioning, giving merchants confidence that flash sales, viral campaigns and global expansion are no longer bounded by platform reliability.

business_problem: Vodafone CZ's commercial teams had to make trading and campaign decisions on day-old data because the on-premises analytics estate was fragmented across a legacy data warehouse, a separate network platform and a separate campaign tool — each with its own integrations — and was operated by external vendors, producing inconsistent figures, costly nine-month upgrade cycles and reports that did not land until afternoon.
solution_summary: Vodafone Czech Republic unified fragmented on-premises data infrastructure by migrating all business performance, network, and campaign data to BigQuery, replacing siloed legacy systems with a single source of truth. Cloud Data Fusion (CDAP) orchestrates data integration, bringing operations in-house and eliminating external vendor dependencies. Data processing now completes by 9 AM, enabling same-day decision-making versus previous day-old reports. The serverless, pay-per-query model reduced operational costs 46% while improving team agility. Teams gained real-time visibility into trading positions, campaign performance, and network metrics, enabling commercial teams to respond to market dynamics within hours instead of days.
business_outcome: Vodafone CZ now operates from a single source of truth with daily data ready before business hours, has eliminated multi-month upgrade outages, is materially cheaper to run on a serverless pay-as-you-go model, and is positioned to migrate ML workloads next.

business_problem: Accenture's enterprise clients wanted to move past generative AI proofs of concept to production-grade, scalable, compliant applications, but ad hoc development stitched together a dozen tools and could not answer how AI would scale reliably, comply with regulations, and be explainable across regulated industries like energy, healthcare, and financial services.
solution_summary: Accenture deployed a production-grade AI platform on Azure AI Foundry that orchestrates responsible AI at enterprise scale. The system ingests enterprise knowledge and client data, leveraging Azure Machine Learning for custom model training and Azure AI Foundry Models for fine-tuning foundational models to specific domains. Azure AI Search enables retrieval-augmented generation for grounded, contextual responses, while Azure Functions and App Service manage application state and event-driven agent workflows. Safety is enforced through layered Azure AI Content Safety filters pre- and post-response, with continuous evaluation across dimensions like groundedness, coherence, and jailbreak resistance. business_problem: Bayer's agronomic advisors and frontline teams were spending hours or days navigating crop-protection labels that can exceed 100 pages, escalating to a small tech team and risking delays and errors when giving precise, label-accurate guidance across products, crops, regions, and regulations.
solution_summary: Bayer developed E.L.Y. Crop Protection (Mini), a domain-specialized small language model built on Microsoft Phi and hosted in Azure AI Foundry. The system ingests proprietary agronomic product label data and regulatory rules, fine-tuned to deliver precise crop protection guidance without hallucination. Frontline agricultural advisors query the model through E.L.Y. Copilot, which retrieves label-accurate answers in real time. Azure AI Foundry provides infrastructure for model versioning, compliance logging, and source-linked traceability. The solution maintains full IP ownership, audit trails, and rapid update cycles to incorporate label changes and regulatory shifts without retraining. Strategic retail partners access the model through secure APIs and access controls, enabling scaled deployment across the United States with measurable productivity gains.
business_outcome: Bayer is seeing 5–10% productivity gains among early users, resolving complex crop-protection questions in under 30 seconds instead of days or weeks, and is planning to expand the model to more retail partners while extending coverage to seeds, biologicals, carbon products, and additional languages.

business_problem: St. Luke’s—with 15 campuses, 300 outpatient sites, 23,000+ employees, and 2.5+ petabytes of patient data—needed unified, real-time visibility across a previously disconnected security stack so its SOC could anticipate and disrupt phishing and DDoS attacks before they paralyzed life-saving care delivery.
solution_summary: St. Luke's University Health Network implemented Microsoft Security Copilot as a unified AI layer across its security operations, connecting Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Sentinel, Microsoft Entra, Microsoft Intune, and Microsoft Purview. The system ingests millions of daily security signals from endpoints, email, identity, applications, and cloud workloads, correlating threats across fragmented platforms. Security Copilot agents including the Security Alert Triage Agent, Vulnerability Remediation Agent, and Conditional Access Optimization Agent autonomously handle routine tasks—triaging phishing emails, categorizing false positives, and remediating vulnerabilities—freeing SOC analysts for proactive threat hunting. All alerts, access controls, and vulnerabilities are consolidated in one interface, providing real-time visibility for faster incident response. Azure Monitor and Log Analytics provide comprehensive audit trails for compliance with healthcare regulations.
business_outcome: St. Luke’s saves nearly 200 hours monthly with the Security Alert Triage Agent autonomously closing thousands of false-positive phishing alerts, reduced incident-report creation from hours to minutes, shifted analysts from reactive triage to proactive threat hunting, and uses Security Copilot’s gap analysis to drive its security roadmap.
